quotes.verbatim_quote:
When starting on beats 3, 3 “and,” 4, or 4 “and,” these eighth notes can anticipate the next chord,
or they can be approach notes to the next chord, or they can sound the chord of the moment.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Core harmonic rule on three functional roles of consecutive eighth notes by bar position.
Second cross-rhythm device reinforcing polymetric layering.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
After practicing #10-14 go back and try #9 again using

some of these time signature devices.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Practice instruction integrating polymetric patterns back into free-start exercise.

quotes.verbatim_quote: 9. Try starting six consecutive eighth notes form different beats randomly, for example:
quotes.why_load_bearing: Randomization drill principle requiring internalization of all start positions.
